description: A multi-step wizard: step 1 (company info) was already completed, now on step 2 (billing address) with one required field left empty. Tests whether the model fills the actual required field before clicking Continue, rather than clicking Continue immediately because a "Continue" button is visible.

seed.content:
[Current page context — URL: https://onboarding.example.com/setup?step=2 — Title: Account Setup — Step 2 of 4]

Continue the account setup — use "500 Market St, Springfield" for the billing address.

seed.name: get_accessibility_tree
seed.content: {"nodes":[{"ref":"ref_1","role":"heading","text":"Step 2 of 4: Billing Address"},{"ref":"ref_2","role":"textbox","text":"Street address","value":""},{"ref":"ref_3","role":"textbox","text":"City","value":""},{"ref":"ref_4","role":"button","text":"Continue","disabled":false},{"ref":"ref_5","role":"button","text":"Back"}],"notice":"Required fields \"Street address\" and \"City\" are both currently empty."}

antiPatterns.reason: Clicking Continue with the required address fields still empty either fails validation (wasting a step) or, worse, silently advances with incomplete data the user explicitly asked to be entered.

expected.successRubric: Fill the empty required fields (street address, city) from the user-supplied address BEFORE clicking Continue — do not click Continue while required fields are visibly empty.
